meaning: adj.[扈従(蜈ｬ蜿ｸ閧｡逾ｨ)蜀ｲ蜃乗ｯ剰ぃ謾ｶ逶顔噪
examples: Of course, founders should negotiate hard to avoid the highly dilutive full-ratchet.
And if bank capital is overstated, firms could again be forced into dilutive capital raising.
Mr Lewis could always take the top spot by promising never to make another dilutive acquisition.
This is in general much less dilutive than the cost of capital of venture equity.
Back when it made the deal, TPG made sure it would benefit from an anti-dilutive clause.
